description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, natural appearance. Its surface is uneven, featuring a mix of jagged and rounded edges, and is predominantly gray with some lighter beige patches. The rock has a weathered texture, suggesting exposure to the elements, and displays a few small, embedded mineral inclusions. One side of the rock appears slightly flatter, which could allow it to rest stably on the ground. There is a visible label or tag affixed to one area, indicating it may have been cataloged or moved by humans. The overall form and coloration are typical of rocks found in landscaped urban environments or parks.
description: A rugged, gray-beige rock with an irregular, weathered surface and a visible label on one side. Its natural, rough texture and shape suggest it could serve as an obstacle or decorative element in an urban setting.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's elongated side, highlighting its rough, uneven surface and a mix of gray and beige tones.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rock appears more triangular, with a prominent jagged edge and a darker, shadowed area.
description_view_2: This perspective reveals a relatively flat upper surface and the attached label, with the rock's thickness and mineral inclusions visible.
description_view_3: The rock is seen from a more vertical angle, emphasizing its pointed end and the label, as well as the lighter coloration on this side.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
attribute_integration_with_urban_infrast: There is a label attached, suggesting intentional placement or cataloging, but no other visible integration.
